subject: Statistics
title: The Efficient Market in Sports
description: One-minute skill: The Efficient Market in Sports.

hook.text: Betting markets predict game outcomes better than most experts. The crowd is smarter than the pundit.

buildup.text: Millions of dollars flow into betting markets, with sharp bettors correcting any mispriced lines.

discovery.text: Closing lines — the final odds before a game — are remarkably well-calibrated as probability estimates.

twist.text: If betting markets are efficient, consistently beating them is nearly impossible. Most bettors lose long-term.

climax.text: Sports betting markets are among the most efficient prediction markets in the world.

punchline.text: The market knows more than any single bettor. Respect the line.

quiz.question: Why are closing betting lines considered accurate probability estimates?
options: Millions of dollars and sharp bettors constantly correct any mispriced lines
Bookmakers have perfect information about every game
Closing lines are set by computer algorithms with no human input
